constraints: CON-001: The system shall parse source code in Java, Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, C, and C++
CON-002: The primary query interface shall conform to the MCP protocol specification
CON-003: All repository indexing shall execute offline in a dedicated cluster; query-time operations shall not trigger repository cloning or source parsing
CON-004: The system shall accept any Git repository accessible via URL as a data source
assumptions: ASM-001: Git repositories are accessible from the indexing cluster via HTTPS or SSH using provided credentials
ASM-002: Programming language of source files can be reliably identified by file extension and parser heuristics
ASM-003: The majority of query traffic originates from AI coding agents rather than human users
ASM-004: Embedding model inference is available as a service or can run locally on the indexing cluster

required_configs.name: PostgreSQL Connection URL
required_configs.description: PostgreSQL connection string for metadata storage

required_configs.name: Redis URL
required_configs.description: Redis connection URL for Celery broker and query cache

required_configs.name: Qdrant URL
required_configs.description: Qdrant vector database URL for semantic search

required_configs.name: Embedding Model
required_configs.description: Embedding model name for semantic search (bge-code)

required_configs.name: Reranker Model
required_configs.description: Reranker model name for neural reranking

required_configs.name: API Key Secret
required_configs.description: Secret key for API key hashing

features.title: Project Skeleton and CI
features.description: M1: Project directory structure, pyproject.toml, CI workflow, core abstractions, storage clients (PostgreSQL, Redis, Qdrant, Elasticsearch)
verification_steps: Given the project root, when checking directory structure, then src/, tests/, docs/, examples/, scripts/ directories exist
Given pyproject.toml exists, when running pip install -e ., then all dependencies install without error
Given .github/workflows/ci.yml exists, when pushed to GitHub, then CI workflow runs pytest and passes
Given storage clients module, when testing PostgreSQL connection with valid DATABASE_URL, then connection succeeds
Given storage clients module, when testing Redis connection with valid REDIS_URL, then ping returns PONG
Given storage clients module, when testing Qdrant connection with valid QDRANT_URL, then health check returns 200
Given storage clients module, when testing Elasticsearch connection with valid ELASTICSEARCH_URL, then cluster health returns green/yellow

features.title: Data Model and Migrations
features.description: M1: SQLAlchemy models for Repository, IndexJob, CodeChunk, APIKey, QueryLog; Alembic migrations
verification_steps: Given a new database, when running alembic upgrade head, then all tables are created (repositories, index_jobs, code_chunks, api_keys, query_logs)
Given the Repository model, when creating a repository record with url, name, languages, then record is persisted and queryable
Given the IndexJob model, when creating a job with status QUEUED, then job is persisted with auto-generated UUID
Given the CodeChunk model, when creating a chunk with repo_id, file_path, content, then record is persisted
Given the APIKey model, when creating a key with hashed key and name, then record is persisted with status active
Given the QueryLog model, when creating a log entry with api_key_id, query_text, latency_ms, then record is persisted with correlation_id

features.title: Repository Registration (FR-001)
features.description: M2: Admin API to register Git repositories with URL, name, and target languages; store metadata in PostgreSQL
verification_steps: Given a valid Git repository URL https://github.com/example/repo.git, when POST /api/v1/repos with url, name, languages=[Java], then response 201 with repository object having status registered
Given an invalid Git URL (not reachable), when POST /api/v1/repos, then response 400 with error message indicating URL is invalid or unreachable
Given an existing repository URL, when POST /api/v1/repos with same URL, then response 409 Conflict
Given registered repositories exist, when GET /api/v1/repos, then response 200 with list of all repositories

features.title: Git Clone or Update (FR-002)
features.description: M2: Celery task to clone repository if not exists, or fetch latest changes if already cloned; handle auth failures
verification_steps: Given a registered repository not yet cloned, when indexing job runs, then full git clone is performed to workspace directory
Given a previously cloned repository, when indexing job runs, then git fetch retrieves only latest changes
Given a repository with invalid credentials, when clone is attempted, then job is marked failed with error message and existing index is preserved
Given a repository with network timeout, when clone is attempted, then job is marked failed after retry attempts exhausted

features.title: Content Extraction (FR-003)
features.description: M2: Identify and extract indexable content from cloned repository: README, source files, CHANGELOG
verification_steps: Given a cloned repository with README.md, src/main.java, and CHANGELOG.md, when content extraction runs, then all three content types are identified and queued for chunking
Given a cloned repository with only binary files, when content extraction runs, then warning is logged and job completes with zero chunks
Given a cloned repository with 100 source files, when content extraction runs, then all 100 files are identified by language extension

features.title: Code Chunking with tree-sitter (FR-004)
features.description: M2: Segment source code into multi-granularity chunks (file, class, function, symbol) using tree-sitter parsers for 6 languages
verification_steps: Given a Java file with 2 classes each containing 3 methods, when chunking executes, then 1 file-level + 2 class-level + 6 method-level chunks are generated
Given a Python file with 4 functions and 2 classes, when chunking executes, then file, class, and function chunks are generated with correct line ranges
Given a source file in unsupported language (e.g., Ruby), when chunking executes, then single file-level text chunk is created without symbol decomposition
Given a TypeScript file with interfaces and type definitions, when chunking executes, then symbol-level chunks include type information

features.title: Embedding Generation and Index Writing
features.description: M2: Generate embeddings for code chunks using bge-code model; write chunks to Elasticsearch (content + metadata) and Qdrant (vectors)
verification_steps: Given 100 code chunks, when embedding generation runs, then 100 embedding vectors are produced with correct dimension
Given embeddings and chunks, when writing to indices, then Elasticsearch contains 100 documents with content, metadata, and chunk_id
Given embeddings and chunks, when writing to indices, then Qdrant collection contains 100 points with same chunk_ids
Given an existing repository being re-indexed, when writing new chunks, then old chunks for that repo_id are deleted first

features.title: Keyword Retrieval (FR-008)
features.description: M3: Retrieve candidate chunks from Elasticsearch using BM25 keyword matching
verification_steps: Given query 'WebClient timeout' and indexed chunks containing 'WebClient.builder().responseTimeout()', when keyword retrieval executes, then matching chunks appear in results
Given a query with no keyword matches, when keyword retrieval executes, then empty candidate list is returned
Given query with repo filter, when keyword retrieval executes, then only chunks from specified repo are returned
Given query with language filter, when keyword retrieval executes, then only chunks in specified language are returned

features.title: Semantic Retrieval (FR-009)
features.description: M3: Retrieve candidate chunks from Qdrant using embedding-based similarity search with configurable threshold (default 0.6)
verification_steps: Given query 'how to configure spring http client timeout' and indexed chunks about WebClient timeout, when semantic retrieval executes, then semantically related chunks appear despite keyword mismatch
Given a query with no semantic matches above threshold 0.6, when semantic retrieval executes, then empty candidate list is returned
Given threshold configured to 0.8, when semantic retrieval executes, then only chunks with similarity >= 0.8 are returned
Given query with repo filter, when semantic retrieval executes, then only chunks from specified repo are returned

features.title: Rank Fusion (FR-010)
features.description: M3: Merge keyword and semantic results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60
verification_steps: Given keyword results [A, B, C] and semantic results [B, D, E], when fusion executes, then merged list contains all 5 unique chunks (A, B, C, D, E) with B benefiting from dual appearance
Given keyword results empty and semantic results [A, B], when fusion executes, then fused list contains [A, B]
Given both retrieval methods return empty, when fusion executes, then empty list is returned

features.title: Neural Reranking (FR-011)
features.description: M3: Reorder fused candidates using bge-reranker cross-encoder; achieve nDCG@3 >= 0.7
verification_steps: Given a fused candidate list with >= 2 items, when reranking executes, then candidates are reordered by neural relevance score
Given a fused candidate list with < 2 items, when reranking executes, then items are returned in current order without model invocation
Given evaluation dataset queries, when reranking is applied, then nDCG@3 >= 0.7 on held-out set

features.title: Context Response Builder (FR-012)
features.description: M3: Build top-3 response with repository, path, symbol, score, and content for each result
verification_steps: Given 50 ranked candidates after reranking, when response is built, then exactly 3 results are returned with fields: repository, path, symbol, score, content
Given zero candidates after full pipeline, when response is built, then empty results array is returned with no error
Given results with varying scores, when response is built, then results are ordered by score descending

features.title: Query Handler - Natural Language (FR-005)
features.description: M3: Accept natural language query text and initiate retrieval pipeline; validate non-empty input
verification_steps: Given natural language query 'how to use spring WebClient timeout', when submitted to QueryHandler, then query is accepted and retrieval pipeline initiated
Given empty query string, when submitted to QueryHandler, then validation error is returned indicating query must not be empty
Given query with only whitespace, when submitted to QueryHandler, then validation error is returned

features.title: Query Handler - Symbol Query (FR-006)
features.description: M3: Accept code symbol identifier as query; validate non-whitespace input
verification_steps: Given symbol query 'org.springframework.web.client.RestTemplate', when submitted to QueryHandler, then query is accepted and retrieval pipeline initiated
Given symbol query containing only whitespace, when submitted, then validation error is returned

features.title: Query Handler - Repository Scoped (FR-007)
features.description: M3: Accept query with target repository filter; restrict retrieval to that repository
verification_steps: Given query 'timeout' scoped to repository 'spring-framework', when retrieval executes, then only chunks from spring-framework are processed
Given query scoped to non-existent repository, when retrieval executes, then empty result set is returned with no error

features.title: API Key Authentication (FR-018)
features.description: M4: Verify valid API key before processing queries; return 401 for invalid/missing keys
verification_steps: Given request with valid API key in X-API-Key header, when auth middleware checks, then request proceeds to handler
Given request with invalid API key, when auth middleware checks, then 401 Unauthorized is returned
Given request with missing API key header, when auth middleware checks, then 401 Unauthorized is returned
Given request with revoked API key, when auth middleware checks, then 401 Unauthorized is returned

features.title: REST API Endpoints
features.description: M4: FastAPI routes for POST /api/v1/query, GET /api/v1/query, repo management, health, metrics
verification_steps: Given valid API key, when POST /api/v1/query with query body, then 200 with QueryResponse
Given valid API key, when GET /api/v1/query?text=foo, then 200 with QueryResponse
Given no auth, when GET /api/v1/health, then 200 with health status
Given no auth, when GET /api/v1/metrics, then Prometheus-format metrics returned

features.title: MCP Server (FR-013)
features.description: M4: Expose search_code_context tool via MCP protocol (stdio and HTTP SSE transport)
verification_steps: Given valid MCP tool-call request with query and api_key, when processed, then structured context results returned as MCP tool response
Given malformed MCP request missing required parameters, when received, then MCP-compliant error response with missing fields indication
Given MCP client connected via stdio, when tool call is sent, then response is received via same transport
Given MCP client connected via HTTP SSE, when tool call is sent, then response is received via SSE

features.title: Web UI Search Page (FR-014)
features.description: M4: Interactive search interface with syntax-highlighted results; Developer Dark theme; requires authentication
verification_steps: [devtools] /search | EXPECT: search input with placeholder, language filter chips (All, Java, Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, C, C++), result card structure | REJECT: console errors, placeholder 'TODO', broken images
Given authenticated user on Web UI, when entering query 'WebClient timeout' and submitting, then results are displayed with syntax-highlighted code snippets and metadata
Given unauthenticated access to Web UI, when search is attempted, then redirect to login page
Given search results displayed, when viewing a result card, then file path, symbol, score badge, and code snippet are visible

features.title: Language Filter (FR-015)
features.description: M4: Restrict retrieval results to specified programming language
verification_steps: Given query 'timeout' with language filter 'Java', when retrieval executes, then only Java-language chunks appear in results
Given query with language filter 'UnsupportedLang', when submitted, then validation error lists supported languages (Java, Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, C, C++)
[devtools] /search | click Python chip → EXPECT: chip selected, other chips deselected | search → EXPECT: only Python results | REJECT: console errors, chips unclickable, wrong language results

features.title: Web UI Login Page (FR-018)
features.description: M4: API key input form; validate and create session; redirect to search on success
verification_steps: [devtools] /login | EXPECT: API key input (password-masked), Sign In button | REJECT: console errors, visible API key value
Given login page, when entering valid API key and clicking Sign In, then redirect to /search and user session established
Given login page, when entering invalid API key and clicking Sign In, then error message 'Invalid API key' appears below input
Given login page, when clicking Sign In with empty input, then validation error appears

features.title: Scheduled Index Refresh (FR-016)
features.description: M5: Celery Beat triggers re-indexing on configurable schedule (default weekly)
verification_steps: Given default configuration, when one week has elapsed since last indexing, then re-indexing is automatically triggered for all registered repositories
Given custom schedule configured to daily, when one day has elapsed, then re-indexing is triggered
Given scheduled re-indexing with some repos failing, when run completes, then successful repos are indexed, errors are logged, and alert is sent

features.title: Manual Reindex Trigger (FR-017)
features.description: M5: Admin API to queue immediate indexing job for specific repository
verification_steps: Given registered repository with no active job, when POST /api/v1/repos/{id}/reindex, then new indexing job is queued and begins processing
Given repository with active indexing job in progress, when POST /api/v1/repos/{id}/reindex, then 409 Conflict with message indicating active job exists

features.title: Metrics Endpoint (NFR-008)
features.description: M5: Prometheus metrics for latency, throughput, index size, error rate
verification_steps: Given running Query Service, when GET /api/v1/metrics, then Prometheus-format response includes ccr_query_latency_seconds, ccr_query_throughput_total, ccr_index_size_bytes, ccr_query_errors_total
Given 100 queries executed, when checking metrics, then ccr_query_throughput_total shows 100 increment

features.title: Query Logging (NFR-009)
features.description: M5: Log all query requests with timestamp, correlation ID, query text, latency
verification_steps: Given 100 queries submitted, when checking logs, then 100 log entries exist each with correlation_id, timestamp, query_text, latency_ms
Given a query fails, when checking logs, then error is logged with same correlation_id for tracing

features.title: NFR-001: Query Latency P95
features.description: P95 query response time <= 1000ms under 1000 QPS load
verification_steps: Given k6 load test with 1000 concurrent users for 10 minutes, when measuring P95 latency, then P95 <= 1000ms

features.title: NFR-002: Query Throughput
features.description: Sustained >= 1000 QPS, peak >= 2000 QPS for 10 minutes
verification_steps: Given k6 load test targeting 1000 QPS for 10 minutes, when measuring throughput, then sustained >= 1000 QPS
Given k6 load test targeting 2000 QPS burst, when measuring peak, then peak >= 2000 QPS achieved

features.title: NFR-003: Repository Capacity
features.description: Index 100-1000 repositories simultaneously without degradation
verification_steps: Given progressive addition of repositories from 100 to 1000, when measuring query latency, then latency remains within NFR-001 bounds

features.title: NFR-004: Single Repository Size
features.description: Index repositories up to 1GB without failure
verification_steps: Given 1GB repository, when indexing job runs, then job completes successfully with all chunks indexed

features.title: NFR-005: Service Availability
features.description: 99.9% uptime (<= 8.76 hours downtime per year)
verification_steps: Given 1-minute health check monitoring, when measuring over measurement period, then 99.9% of health checks succeed

features.title: NFR-006: Linear Scalability
features.description: Adding a node increases throughput linearly (±20%)
verification_steps: Given N query nodes, when measuring throughput, then N+1 nodes provide 80-120% of per-node capacity increase

features.title: NFR-007: Single-Node Failure Tolerance
features.description: Zero query failures during single-node outage; failover <= 30s
verification_steps: Given multi-node deployment with load balancer, when one query node is killed, then zero queries fail and failover completes within 30s
